perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Cargill and Bioamber entered into a Commercial License Agreement having an Effective Date of April 15, 2010 (the "Commercial License"); WHEREAS, Bioamber now desires to amend the Development Agreement to allow Bioamber to fund a research project being conducted by the Biotechnology Research Institute ("BRI"), which involves the molecular re-engineering of a Methylotroph owned by BRI and the development of a lab scale fermentation design for using the re-engineered Methylotroph to make succinic acid or salts thereof from a methanol feedstock (the "BRI Project"). The BRI Project will be co-funded by funds available from the Canadian National Research Council; WHEREAS, Bioamber further desires to scale-up the production of succinic acid using a Corynebacteria biocatalyst (MCC-17) available from Mitsubishi Chemical Corporation ("MCC") and to possibly produce succinic acid or salts using MCC-17 as an alternative to the E. coli BioAmber has licensed from the DOE at: (1) Bioamber's existing demonstration-scale succinic acid production facility located at Pomacle, France; and (2) a succinic acid production facility located at Sarnia, Ontario Canada having a maximum production capacity of 35,000 metric tons of succinic acid per year (the "Sarnia Plant"). Together these scale-up projects will be referred to as the "Scale-up and Production Project"; WHEREAS, Cargill is willing to allow Bioamber to fund the BRI Project and to conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to the following terms and conditions.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Cargill and Bioamber entered into a Commercial License Agreement having an Effective Date of April 15, 2010 (the "Commercial License"); WHEREAS, Bioamber now desires to amend the Development Agreement to allow Bioamber to fund a research project being conducted by the Biotechnology Research Institute ("BRI"), which involves the molecular re-engineering of a Methylotroph owned by BRI and the development of a lab scale fermentation design for using the re-engineered Methylotroph to make succinic acid or salts thereof from a methanol feedstock (the "BRI Project"). The BRI Project will be co-funded by funds available from the Canadian National Research Council; WHEREAS, Bioamber further desires to scale-up the production of succinic acid using a Corynebacteria biocatalyst (MCC-17) available from Mitsubishi Chemical Corporation ("MCC") and to possibly produce succinic acid or salts using MCC-17 as an alternative to the E. coli BioAmber has licensed from the DOE at: (1) Bioamber's existing demonstration-scale succinic acid production facility located at Pomacle, France; and (2) a succinic acid production facility located at Sarnia, Ontario Canada having a maximum production capacity of 35,000 metric tons of succinic acid per year (the "Sarnia Plant"). Together these scale-up projects will be referred to as the "Scale-up and Production Project";
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'WHEREAS, Cargill is willing to allow Bioamber to fund the BRI Project and to conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to the following terms and conditions,' we are omitting the explicit agreement from Cargill to allow Bioamber to conduct these projects. This could introduce legal risk if Cargill later disputes Bioamber's right to conduct these projects, as the contract no longer clearly states Cargill's consent and the obligations for Bioamber to follow the existing terms and conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract law requires mutual consent and clear terms. By removing Cargill's explicit willingness and stating that projects are 'subject to the following terms and conditions', the modification introduces ambiguity, which could contradict the fundamental principle of offer and acceptance required for contract formation, potentially violating contract law principles.
perturbation.law_explanation: The deletion of Cargill's willingness introduces uncertainty regarding the project's permissibility and applicable conditions. This contrasts with contract law requirements for clarity and mutual consent. The Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 17 mandates a 'bargain in which there is a manifestation of mutual assent to the exchange'. The modification raises concerns about the existence of mutual assent, potentially undermining the contract's validity under general contract law principles.
perturbation.location: Beginning of 'Amendment To the Development Agreement' section
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 2-204. Formation in General. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-204. Formation in General. (1) A contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement , including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract . (2) An agreement sufficient to constitute a contract for sale may be found even though the moment of its making is undetermined. (3) Even though one or more terms are left open a contract for sale does not fail for indefiniteness if the parties have intended to make a contract and there is a reasonably certain basis for giving an appropriate remedy. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original text states Cargill is 'willing to allow Bioamber to fund the BRI Project and to conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to the following terms and conditions.' The changed text removes this statement of willingness. The Perturbation Explanation states that this omission introduces risk because Cargill's consent is no longer clearly stated, and the Law Explanation states that this undermines the contract's validity under general contract law principles. The Uniform Commercial Code snippet provided states that a contract for the sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement. By removing Cargill's explicit agreement, a contradiction arises because the contract's demonstration of agreement is weakened. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
